cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the letter carefully to understand what Carmen Rivera is trying to communicate. The letter starts with Carmen saying that her family had an enjoyable holiday at the Magic Kingdom - Disneyland, and that one event made their visit "truly magical." This event involved their daughter, Gloria, who was upset about not being able to take her ice-cream on the Space Mountain ride. A staff member named Murphy helped Gloria by holding onto her ice-cream and promising to give it back after the ride. However, since the ice-cream would melt in the Florida heat, Murphy actually bought a new one just before they exited the ride. Carmen then writes, "We know that [blank] tried hard to make our visit special. Thanks so much for going above and beyond!" This sentence clearly indicates that the purpose of the letter is to express gratitude for the staff member's efforts to make their visit special and magical. Now, let's look at the question: "Carmen Rivera wrote the letter to [blank]." The options are: (A) order ice-creams (B) promise to visit again (C) express her thanks (D) give some suggestions Option (A) doesn't make sense in the context of the letter. Carmen is not ordering ice-creams; she is recalling an incident. Option (B) is also not supported by the text. There's no indication in the letter that Carmen is promising to visit again, although it's possible that the positive experience might make them want to return, it's not the purpose of the letter. Option (C) is directly supported by the letter. Carmen is thanking the park, specifically mentioning the staff member's efforts to go "above and beyond" to make their visit special.
